evals.prompt: Use smoketest-changes to sync flows from this branch.
evals.expected_output: Should first check that the Smoketest CLI is installed and authenticated, helping the user install @smoketest.sh/cli and run smoketest auth login or smoketest init if needed. After CLI setup succeeds, it should inspect existing Smoketest projects, flows, subflows, and environments, collect git changes against origin/main or main plus working tree changes, inspect changed route/UI/test/doc files, draft .smoketest/changes/manifest.json and flow markdown that verifies stable changed functionality rather than exact mutable copy, validate and dry-run the manifest, include a short contextual dashboard tip, and ask before CLI mutation.
assertions: Checks Smoketest CLI install and auth before change analysis
Uses branch diff against origin/main or main by default
Includes staged, unstaged, and untracked relevant files
Drafts resilient flow steps that avoid exact marketing, blog, changelog, date, count, price, or latest-title assertions
Mentions a relevant Smoketest web dashboard surface

evals.prompt: Use smoketest-changes for origin/main...HEAD and update existing Smoketest coverage.
evals.expected_output: Should use the explicit git range, inspect current Smoketest flows through the CLI, choose update only for exact-name matches, choose create for newly discovered journeys, skip near duplicates and content-only changes that do not affect durable user journeys, and ask before applying any create/update actions.
assertions: Skips or generalizes content-only changes instead of encoding exact page copy

evals.prompt: Recent changes removed a product route. Use smoketest-changes.
evals.expected_output: Should report removed routes as possible stale Smoketest coverage, use skip for any deleted journey candidate, avoid deleting Smoketest resources, and ask whether the user wants separate cleanup guidance.
assertions: Separates cleanup guidance from flow apply

evals.prompt: Use smoketest-changes for changed authenticated dashboard flows.
evals.expected_output: Should require installed and authenticated CLI before environment work, select or create a Smoketest environment, store passwords only through masked CLI prompts such as smoketest env set <env> TEST_USER_PASSWORD --secret, draft or update a login subflow when needed, attach environment to authenticated candidates, mention dashboard environment or authenticated run review surfaces when relevant, and ask before applying.
assertions: Requires CLI auth before environment setup
Never asks for passwords in chat
Drafts reusable login setup when useful
Mentions dashboard environment or authenticated run review surfaces
